evals.name: debian-deep-analysis
evals.prompt: My Debian 14 machine panicked at boot and showed a QR code. The kernel crash report is in assets/debian-oops.txt. Please give me a thorough root-cause analysis: what exactly crashed and why, how the bug could have reached that state, and where a fix should go. Also flag anything unusual you notice in the report.
evals.expected_output: The analysis identifies that rcu_do_batch called a NULL function pointer (RIP=0x0) stored in the rcu_head.func field of a kernfs_node object. The slab debug line is used to identify the owning object. CR2=0xffffffffffffffd6 is explained as the kernel's failed attempt to read opcode bytes 42 bytes before RIP=0. Taint W (prior WARNING) is flagged as potentially the root trigger. A What-How-Where structure is present.
expectations: Root cause conclusion names rcu_do_batch as the crashing function and identifies a NULL function pointer (RIP=0x0 or func=NULL) as the mechanism

The agent obtains and uses debug symbols from the Debian linux-image-*-dbg package — either by finding them pre-staged in the workspace (e.g., referencing a vmlinux path under oops-workdir/debian/ or usr/lib/debug/boot/) or by providing explicit download commands — and produces gdb or addr2line output that resolves at least one backtrace offset to a source file and line number from those symbols
Source code for rcu_do_batch (or the inlined function at the crash offset) is shown, including at least the relevant call or crash line
CR2=0xffffffffffffffd6 is explained in relation to RIP=0x0, not treated as an unrelated fault address
The slab debug line is referenced and used to connect the RDI register value to a kernfs_node object
Taint flag W (prior WARNING) is flagged as significant — the earlier WARNING may be the actual root cause
The report has a structured What / How / Where (or equivalent) analysis rather than just extraction of fields

evals.name: fedora-deep-analysis
evals.prompt: My Fedora 43 laptop crashed while the iio-sensor-prox process was running. The crash report is in assets/fedora-oops.txt. Please give me a thorough root-cause analysis: what exactly faulted and why, how the driver ended up in that state, and where the fix should go. Flag anything unusual in the report.
evals.expected_output: The analysis identifies that bmc150_accel_set_interrupt read a NULL info pointer (crash address 0x1 = struct member at offset 1 from NULL). The root cause is that bmc150_accel_interrupts_setup was never called because the device has no IRQ, so the info pointer was never initialized. A fix is proposed. CR2=0x1 is noted as a struct member offset rather than a true NULL dereference.
expectations: Root cause conclusion names bmc150_accel_set_interrupt as the crashing function and identifies a NULL or uninitialized info pointer as the mechanism
CR2=0x1 (fault address 0x1) is correctly interpreted as reading from a struct member at offset 1 of a NULL pointer, not as a NULL dereference itself
Source code for bmc150_accel_set_interrupt is shown including at least the line that dereferences the info pointer
Analysis explains that the interrupts array was never initialized because the device has no IRQ (bmc150_accel_interrupts_setup not called on the no-IRQ path)
A concrete fix suggestion is provided (e.g. NULL check for info before use, or a guard in the caller)
Taint flag W (prior WARNING) is noted

The agent demonstrates use of the kernel-debuginfo RPM debug symbols — either by running gdb against a pre-staged .ko.debug file to resolve a backtrace offset to source, or by explicitly acknowledging the RPM is available (pre-staged or downloaded from kojipkgs) and describing its contents (.ko.debug module files, no vmlinux) before falling back to source-tree analysis. Simply proceeding without any reference to the RPM or its debug files is not sufficient.

Every source code line in source-reporting sections (backtrace source mapping, crash location, related function listings) has a numeric line number prefix (or ~NNN approximation) — diff or patch blocks are exempt from this requirement

The agent identifies that this bug is already fixed upstream by commit 3aa385a9c75c09b59dcab2ff76423439d23673ab (iio: accel: bmc150: Fix irq assumption regression, by Linus Walleij) and notes that this fix was not yet present in the affected kernel version

evals.name: testoops-warning
evals.prompt: I upgraded my Arch Linux system from kernel 6.14.7-arch2-1 to 6.15.1-arch1-2 and now I get this kernel warning at boot (assets/testoops.txt). It was completely fine before the upgrade. Please analyze this: classify the event, identify the source location, show me the relevant source code, and flag anything unusual in the report.
evals.expected_output: The event is correctly classified as a WARNING (not Oops/Panic/BUG). The source location ucsi.c:1380 is extracted directly from the WARNING header. The build ID hash in the typec_ucsi backtrace entry is correctly identified as a hash, not a second module name. OOT and unsigned module taint flags (O, E from nvidia modules) are noted. Source code for ucsi_reset_ppm is shown.
expectations: Event is classified as WARNING (WARN/WARN_ON), not as Oops, Panic, or BUG
Source location ucsi.c:1380 (or drivers/usb/typec/ucsi/ucsi.c:1380) is extracted and attributed to the ucsi_reset_ppm entry in the backtrace, without needing vmlinux
The build ID hash after typec_ucsi in the ucsi_init_work backtrace entry is correctly identified as a build hash, not a second module name
Taint flags O (out-of-tree) and E (unsigned) are noted, with nvidia identified as the source
Source code for ucsi_reset_ppm (or the WARNING call site in ucsi.c around line 1380) is shown

evals.name: lkml-bug-filemap-fault
evals.prompt: I got this kernel crash report from the LKML mailing list (assets/lkml-oops.txt). It's from a Cloudflare production machine. Please give me a thorough root-cause analysis: what exactly asserted and why, how the kernel ended up in that state, and where a fix should go. Flag anything unusual in the report.
evals.expected_output: The analysis identifies a VM_BUG_ON_FOLIO assertion in filemap_fault (mm/filemap.c:3519) where a folio retrieved from the XFS page cache does not contain the requested page index. The root cause is a TOCTOU race: a concurrent truncation/reclaim clears folio->mapping to NULL between the truncation guard (line 3514) and the assertion (line 3519). The folio.mapping=0x0 evidence from crash tool is cited. Source code for filemap_fault showing lines 3514 and 3519 is shown. The custom Cloudflare kernel triggers use of the general source tree fallback. A defensive fix (convert VM_BUG_ON_FOLIO to retry) is proposed.
expectations: Event is correctly classified as BUG/BUG_ON (specifically VM_BUG_ON_FOLIO), not as a paging request Oops or Panic
Root cause conclusion identifies a race condition (TOCTOU or concurrent truncation/reclaim) between the truncation guard at filemap_fault line 3514 and the assertion at line 3519

The analysis identifies that the folio retrieved from the page cache does not contain the requested index because of a race during large-folio splitting or truncation — not simply because folio.mapping is NULL (which is a post-crash artifact, not the cause)
Source code for filemap_fault (mm/filemap.c) is shown including both the truncation guard and the VM_BUG_ON_FOLIO assertion lines, with numeric line numbers
The custom Cloudflare kernel (no standard distro debug packages) causes the agent to use the general source tree fallback (oops-workdir/linux or equivalent) rather than distro-specific download steps
A fix is proposed: either converting VM_BUG_ON_FOLIO to a soft retry (like the truncation check above it) or hardening the truncation guard
The index mismatch (page dump shows index 0x7652 while the faulted index is 0x7653) is noted as an unusual or significant observation

The agent identifies that this bug is already fixed upstream by commit 577a1f495fd78 (mm: huge_memory: pass old_folio to xas_try_split, by Zi Yan, 2026-03-02) and notes that the fix is in __split_unmapped_folio in mm/huge_memory.c

evals.name: ubuntu-gfs2-null-deref
evals.prompt: I'm running Ubuntu on a QEMU VM and stress-ng caused this kernel crash (assets/ubuntu-oops2.txt). Please give me a full root-cause analysis: what crashed and why, how the kernel ended up in that state, and where a fix should go.
evals.expected_output: The analysis identifies a NULL pointer dereference in gfs2_trans_add_revoke where current->journal_info (the active GFS2 transaction pointer, tr) is NULL. The fault instruction lock orb $0x2, 0x18(%rbx) writes to tr->tr_flags (offset 0x18) with RBX=0. The root cause is that do_gfs2_set_flags calls truncate_inode_pages before gfs2_trans_begin, so when journaled-data pages trigger gfs2_trans_add_revoke during page invalidation there is no open transaction. The fix should move truncate_inode_pages to after gfs2_trans_begin, or add a NULL guard in gfs2_trans_add_revoke. The agent correctly identifies this as Ubuntu from the '#14-Ubuntu' build string (not the UNAME alone), handles the missing '~XX.XX.X' version suffix by searching Launchpad to find package version 7.0.0-14.14 (series: resolute), and uses the gfs2 module debug ELF (not vmlinux) for source mapping.
assertions: Ubuntu is correctly identified as the distro from the '#14-Ubuntu' build string, not from the UNAME suffix alone
The agent recognises that the '#14-Ubuntu' build string has no '~XX.XX.X' part and follows the fallback procedure rather than applying the standard version formula
The faulting instruction is decoded as 'lock orb $0x2, 0x18(%rbx)' (or equivalent atomic bit-set), with RBX=0 and fault address 0x18 identified as tr->tr_flags offset in struct gfs2_trans
current->journal_info (the GFS2 transaction pointer tr) being NULL is identified as the immediate cause of the crash
The root cause identifies that do_gfs2_set_flags calls truncate_inode_pages before gfs2_trans_begin, meaning no transaction is open when gfs2_invalidate_folio triggers gfs2_trans_add_revoke
The crash is in the gfs2 loadable module and the agent uses the module debug ELF (gfs2.ko or gfs2.ko.zst decompressed) rather than vmlinux for source mapping
Source code for gfs2_trans_add_revoke (fs/gfs2/trans.c) and do_gfs2_set_flags (fs/gfs2/file.c) is shown, including the relevant lines, obtained from Ubuntu-specific source (not mainline git alone)
A fix is proposed targeting do_gfs2_set_flags in fs/gfs2/file.c — moving truncate_inode_pages to after gfs2_trans_begin, or adding a transaction-active guard before the truncation
